Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.

Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.

Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.

When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The board didn't change. board didn't change. The board_changed=false. So clicking on the sky blue square in the top left did nothing. Let me think about this differently.
Looking at the level 2 layout:
- Top left has 4 colored squares: sky blue, magenta, purple, yellow
- Top bar has a yellow square
- Center has a blue circle
- 4 magenta squares scattered around
- White line at bottom
The game mechanic seems to be: click on the sky blue dots to turn them into magenta squares. But the level isn't completed yet.
Maybe the white line shrank.
Wait, I need to think I need to look at this differently. The top left corner has 4 colored squares that could be a color palette or selection menu. The yellow square in the top bar is the "active" color. The magenta squares on the board are the "player" pieces" that I need to move.
Maybe the goal is to click on the magenta squares to move them to the blue circle?
Let me try clicking on the magenta square (ID=9, at [[36,17] to [37,18]).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try clicking on a magenta square (ID=9, at [[36,17] to [37,18])
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 36, 'col': 17}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
